creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 186480
Sample Makefile

Running `portlint -C` on the attached Makefile, I get

  WARN: Makefile: "EXPIRATION_DATE" has to appear earlier.
  WARN: Makefile: "DEPRECATED" has to appear earlier.
  WARN: Makefile: "EXPIRATION_DATE" has to appear earlier.
  WARN: Makefile: "LIB_DEPENDS" has to appear earlier.
  WARN: Consider to set DEVELOPER=yes in /etc/make.conf

which duplicates the first WARNing.

If I reshuffle the statements to 

  DEPRECATED=     Unsupported by upstream. Use GCC 5 or newer instead.
  EXPIRATION_DATE=        2018-03-31
  ONLY_FOR_ARCHS= amd64 i386 powerpc sparc64

the duplication is gone, though I still get

  WARN: Makefile: "EXPIRATION_DATE" has to appear earlier.
  WARN: Makefile: "ONLY_FOR_ARCHS" has to appear earlier.

Seems I cannot win? :-(

This may be related to bug #222075.
